2006-07-01.title: Germany: Lawmakers Back Federal Overhaul
2006-07-01.abstract: Parliament's lower house approved an overhaul of the complex federal system of government. The changes, which would be the broadest ever to the country's 1949 Constitution and which the upper house is expected to approve next week, would give Germany's 16 states more say in issues like schools and civil service. In exchange, the states' veto powers in Parliament would be curbed to speed decision-making. The number of laws that require the approval of the upper house would be cut to less than 40 percent, from about 60 percent now.

2006-07-01.title: Congo Nears Historic Election, Praying for Peace
2006-07-01.abstract: Congo's biggest challenge will be getting its competing groups to recognize and respect the election results.

2006-07-01.title: The Hague: Muslim Freed After Conviction
2006-07-01.abstract: The International War Crimes Tribunal sentenced Naser Oric, the wartime commander of the Muslim force at Srebrenica, to two years imprisonment for failing to prevent the cruel treatment and killing of Serbian prisoners during Bosnia's 1992-95 civil war. He was immediately released because he had already spent three years in detention. Prosecutors had asked for an 18-year sentence, for the looting and burning of Serb villages by the poorly trained volunteer force under his command. But the judges said the

2006-07-01.title: G.I.'s Investigated in Slayings of 4 and Rape in Iraq
2006-07-01.abstract: The investigation is the fourth into suspected killings of unarmed Iraqis by American soldiers announced by the military in June.

2006-07-01.title: Captured Soldier Is Described as a Gentle Basketball Fan
2006-07-01.abstract: TEL AVIV, June 30 — In a photograph reprinted daily on Israel's front pages, Cpl. Gilad Shalit, 19, is seen with a wide grin, his smooth, boyish face dominated by a pair of large round glasses, his army uniform hanging loosely on his thin frame.

2006-07-02.title: Suddenly, Sand Bags and Potshots at Post 1
2006-07-02.abstract: On a lonely night on a rooftop in Ramadi, Lance Cpl. Joseph Hamlin talked about his life.

2006-07-02.abstract: The main Sunni Arab political bloc started a boycott of parliament to protest the kidnapping of a Sunni legislator.

2006-07-02.title: Israeli Airstrike Over Gaza Hits Premier's Office
2006-07-02.abstract: Israel stepped up its campaign to pressure the Palestinians for the release of an Israeli soldier, who was said to be in stable condition.

2006-07-02.title: Israel Squeezes, and Gazans Adapt to the Vise
2006-07-02.abstract: Many Palestinians believe Israel is trying to make them so miserable that they turn against the Hamas government.

2006-07-03.title: China Law Seeks to Curb Foreign Media, Too
2006-07-03.abstract: A Chinese draft law that threatens to fine news media for reporting on "sudden incidents" without permission applies to foreign as well as domestic news organizations.

2006-07-03.title: Israel Steps Up Raids in Bid to Free Soldier
2006-07-03.abstract: Israel intensified its attacks on Palestinian targets in Gaza, including the offices of Fatah, but also allowed in a limited supply of fuel and food.

2006-07-03.abstract: The World Cup has not generated the surge in demand for prostitution or the influx of temporary prostitutes that many experts predicted.

2006-07-03.abstract: Senegal agreed to try the former dictator for human rights violations he is accused of committing during his eight-year rule.

2006-07-03.abstract: The contests seemed to have intensified from the conspiracies of home-grown bombers to the poppy-field wars against the Taliban in Afghanistan.

2006-07-04.title: Conservative Has Slight Edge in Mexico Vote
2006-07-04.abstract: Unofficial results in the fiercely contested election for president showed Felipe Calderón, the fiscal conservative, with a lead of one percentage point.

2006-07-04.title: Herty Lewites, 66, Ex-Sandinista, Dies
2006-07-04.abstract: Herty Lewites was a contender in the race for the Nicaraguan presidency and an innovative minister of tourism under the Sandinistas.

2006-07-04.abstract: The "Civil G8" forum opened in Moscow Monday ahead of the Group of Eight summit.

2006-07-04.title: Russia: Putin Warns of Christian-Islam Conflict
2006-07-04.abstract: Christians, Jews, Muslims and Buddhists from several dozen countries including Iran, Israel and the United States have gathered for the World Summit of Religious Leaders, which opened in Moscow. Addressing the opening ceremony, President Vladimir V. Putin warned of the dangers of a standoff between Christianity and Islam and said,

2006-07-04.title: Sri Lanka: At Least 9 Killed in Soaring Violence
2006-07-04.abstract: SRI LANKA: AT LEAST 9 KILLED IN SOARING VIOLENCE Six members of the security forces and a civilian were killed and at least 14 others wounded in a powerful explosion in Trincomalee, an eastern port city. The blast was caused by a bomb placed on a three-wheel taxi and detonated by remote control near a military checkpoint, said Brig. Prasad Samarasinghe, a military spokesman. He blamed the Tamil Tiger rebels for the blast, which wounded mostly civilians. A soldier and a suspected rebel were also killed in separate incidents in northern Sri Lanka. The new violence took place as Shyam Saran, India's foreign secretary, arrived amid concerns for the deteriorating security situation in Sri Lanka.

2006-07-05.title: 2 Italians Arrested in Kidnapping of Radical Cleric
2006-07-05.abstract: It was the first indication that the Italian government may have helped American intelligence agents detain the imam.

2006-07-05.title: Turkey: Publisher Faces Prosecution
2006-07-05.abstract: A publisher who printed a book by the linguist Noam Chomsky was indicted on a charge of

2006-07-05.title: Spain: Officials Say Speed Caused Derailment
2006-07-05.abstract: Spain's worst subway train crash, in which 41 people died, was caused by high speed, officials said, adding that there was a possibility that the driver passed out. Black box data showed the train in Valencia was going at 48 miles per hour, twice the normal speed in the tunnel curve, when it derailed Monday, the city transport chief, José Ramón García, said in a statement.

2006-07-05.title: In Ramadi, Fetid Quarters and Unrelenting Battles
2006-07-05.abstract: U.S. commanders say they are planning to convert about three blocks in the middle of Ramadi into a "Green Zone."

2006-07-05.title: Asian Markets Are Rattled by Launchings
2006-07-05.abstract: Investors reacted to concerns that a diplomatic crisis could slow economic growth in one of the world's hottest regions.

2006-07-06.title: Suspense Grows as Vote Count in Mexico Race Wraps Up
2006-07-06.abstract: The authorities reported that the tally was tilting toward the leftist candidate, Andrés Manuel López Obrador.

2006-07-06.title: North Korea Vows to Continue Missile Tests
2006-07-06.abstract: North Korea today responded to international condemnation with characteristic defiance.

2006-07-06.title: Iran and Europe to Meet Today
2006-07-06.abstract: Iran's chief nuclear negotiator, Ali Larijani, is to meet Javier Solana, the European Union's foreign policy chief.

2006-07-06.title: Leak Disrupts French Terror Trial
2006-07-06.abstract: A leaked report said France secretly interviewed the six defendants while they were detained by the U.S. at Guantánamo Bay.

2006-07-06.title: Iraqi Says Immunity for Soldiers Fosters Crime
2006-07-06.abstract: The prime minister said that Iraq will investigate the recently disclosed rape-and-murder case involving U.S. soldiers.

2006-07-07.title: Ukraine: Coalition Deal Shaken
2006-07-07.abstract: Ukraine's political parties broke a deadlock that had paralyzed the new Parliament for weeks, but doubts remained on the formation of a new government. President Viktor A. Yushchenko's party had agreed to hold votes for speaker and prime minister. But the party's candidate for speaker, Petro O. Poroshenko, withdrew, threatening a deal for his rival, Yulia V. Tymoshenko, to become prime minister. A vote on her candidacy is now uncertain.

2006-07-07.title: Iraq's New Army Uses Creaky Soviet-Era Armored Vehicles to Recreate a Mailed Fist
2006-07-07.abstract: The Russian T-55 tank is more than a relic: it is the building block of the new Iraqi Army's first and only armored division.

2006-07-07.title: London Is Solemn on Anniversary of Blasts
2006-07-07.abstract: Much of London fell silent as Britons observed two minutes silence to mourn the 52 people killed on July 7, 2005.

2006-07-07.title: Fighting Surges and Deaths Rise as Israel Drives Deeper in Gaza
2006-07-07.abstract: Israeli troops and Palestinian militants waged their most intense battles since Israel re-entered Gaza last week.

2006-07-07.title: Russia: Murder Trial Challenged
2006-07-07.abstract: The family of Paul Klebnikov, the editor of the Russian edition of Forbes magazine who was murdered in 2004, filed a complaint with the Supreme Court protesting the case's handling in lower courts. The complaint said a Moscow trial judge had failed to release records of the closed trial after it ended with the acquittal of two suspects on May 5. The family's complaint also said the judge was holding up appeals of the verdict in higher courts.

2006-07-08.title: A Drive to Root Out the Resurgent Taliban
2006-07-08.abstract: American troops are engaged in their biggest operation against Taliban forces in Afghanistan since 2001.

2006-07-08.abstract: Much of London fell silent as Britons observed two minutes of silence to mourn the 52 people killed on July 7, 2005.

2006-07-08.title: Sanctions Lifted, Libya Withholds Final Lockerbie Payment
2006-07-08.abstract: Libya is holding off on making hundreds of millions of dollars in payments to the families of Americans killed on Pan Am 103.

2006-07-08.title: Joint Raid Captures 2 Linked to Rebel Shiite Leader
2006-07-08.abstract: The raids were a rare crackdown by Shiite-led troops against forces linked to Moktada al-Sadr.

2006-07-08.title: Afghan Legislator Accuses U.S.-Led Forces of Firing on His Family
2006-07-08.abstract: Haji Abdul Khaliq said that the shooting occurred on Wednesday and that American and Australian troops were responsible for it.

2006-07-09.title: U.S. Military Braces for Flurry of Criminal Cases in Iraq
2006-07-09.abstract: Military officials say the total of American servicemen charged with capital crimes in the new cases could grow substantially.

2006-07-09.title: Leftist Predicts Unrest Without Complete Recount of Mexican Election
2006-07-09.abstract: Andrés Manuel López Obrador led a rally of at least 150,000 people in Mexico City, charging that the polling had been marred by fraud.

2006-07-09.abstract: An Israeli official said there would be no cease-fire until the release of a kidnapped Israeli soldier.

2006-07-09.abstract: The abduction of Tayseer Najah al-Mashhadani has plunged the new government, led by Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, into its first major political crisis.

2006-07-10.title: Brazen Daytime Violence Continues in Baghdad
2006-07-10.abstract: At least 30 people died today, highlighting the immense challenges facing the new government.

2006-07-10.title: Baghdad Erupts in Mob Violence
2006-07-10.abstract: The Sunni-Shiite bloodletting was frightening even by recent standards. Also, five more U.S. soldiers were accused in a rape and murder case.

2006-07-10.title: At Least 122 Dead in Russian Plane Crash
2006-07-10.abstract: A plane with 204 aboard careened off a rain-slicked runway in Siberia, broke up and burst into flames.

2006-07-10.title: India Reports a Long-Range Missile Test
2006-07-10.abstract: It was unclear if the test was successful, with at least one report saying that the missile had failed in its flight.

2006-07-10.title: Dozens Killed in Pakistani Plane Crash
2006-07-10.abstract: Questions and criticism immediately surfaced about the quality and maintenance of aging planes.

2006-07-11.title: Sudan: Oxfam Closes 2 Darfur Offices
2006-07-11.abstract: The aid group Oxfam closed two of its six offices in the north of the Darfur region because of the abduction of a Sudanese staff member who has been missing for two months. Efforts to track him have been fruitless. Oxfam’s regional director, Paul Smith-Lomas, said it would continue providing aid in the rest of Darfur, but he said lack of security was complicating efforts to reach three million people dependent on aid in the region.

2006-07-11.title: Chechen Rebel's Cause May Die With Him
2006-07-11.abstract: Shamil Basayev's death was the latest of almost two years of setbacks for his separatist faction in Chechnya.

2006-07-11.title: Insurgent Group Posts Video of 2 Mutilated U.S. Soldiers
2006-07-11.abstract: A message with the video said the soldiers were killed out of revenge for the rape and murder of an Iraqi girl in March.

2006-07-11.title: 50 Killed in Baghdad as Iraqi Violence Worsens
2006-07-11.abstract: Attacks included a double suicide attack near the fortified Green Zone, beheadings, shootings, car bombs, mortar attacks and a bus ambush.

2006-07-11.title: Rumsfeld Reassures Afghans in Visit
2006-07-11.abstract: The secretary of defense promised Afghan officials today that the Bush administration would not lose interest in stabilizing Afghanistan.

2006-07-12.title: Britain: Expanding Role for Nuclear Power
2006-07-12.abstract: Foreshadowing a major environmental debate, the British government said nuclear power would make a “significant contribution” to future energy needs. Nuclear power currently accounts for one-fifth of Britain’s energy, scheduled to fall by 6 percent by 2020. Alistair Darling, the trade and industry minister, told Parliament that a new generation of nuclear power stations could help reduce carbon emissions and dependence on imported energy.

2006-07-12.title: Wave of Violence in Baghdad Puts 3-Day Death Toll Past 100
2006-07-12.abstract: Many Iraqis say the worsening security crisis in Baghdad and neighboring provinces feels like a low-grade civil war.

2006-07-12.title: A Platoon’s Mission: Seeking and Destroying Explosives in Disguise
2006-07-12.abstract: Hidden bombs have become Iraqi insurgents’ primary weapon, and the number being planted is on the rise.

2006-07-12.title: Attacks Unlikely to Weigh Down India Economy
2006-07-12.abstract: "There will be zero impact on the economy in tangible, quantitative terms," the managing director of an Indian conglomerate said.

2006-07-12.title: Ukraine President’s Backers Urge a New Election
2006-07-12.abstract: With parliamentary debate in Ukraine reduced to insults and fistfights, supporters called for President Viktor A. Yushchenko to dissolve Parliament.

2006-07-13.title: France: Le Pen Faces Trial for Remarks
2006-07-13.abstract: The far-right leader Jean-Marie Le Pen is headed to court for injudicious comments he made last year about the Nazis’ wartime activities in France. The trial will decide whether he is guilty of “complicity in contesting crimes against humanity and complicity in justifying war crimes” by telling a right-wing weekly magazine last year that “in France, at least, the German occupation was not particularly inhumane, although there were some blunders, inevitable in a country of 550,000 square kilometers.” Mr. Le Pen has been fined twice for dismissing the Holocaust as a “detail” of history. Denying the Holocaust is a crime in France, punishable by fines or prison.

2006-07-13.title: Talks Between North and South Korea Collapse
2006-07-13.abstract: North Korea rejected the South’s call for it to end missile tests, instead countering with a demand for more aid.

2006-07-13.title: Violence Ebbs in Iraq After 4-Day Rampage
2006-07-13.abstract: The government of Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki is scrambling to respond to this week’s surge in violence.

2006-07-13.title: Israel Attacks Beirut Airport and Sets Up Naval Blockade
2006-07-13.abstract: Hezbollah fired rockets deep into northern Israel today, with two rockets hitting the port of Haifa, as the crisis in the Middle East escalated rapidly.

2006-07-13.title: Iran: U.S. Is Asked to Return Ancient Tablets
2006-07-13.abstract: A government official said Iran would use diplomacy and cultural channels to regain ancient clay tablets that a United States court ruled last week could be sold to pay compensation to victims of a 1997 Jerusalem bombing. The tablets date to between 553 B.C. and 330 B.C. and are the oldest Persian tablets with alphabetical inscriptions. They have been under study at the University of Chicago’s Oriental Institute since the 1930’s. The University of Chicago has opposed the court ruling and called the tablets an “irreplaceable scholarly data set” that should not be subject to political battles. Iran’s Foreign Ministry also denounced the court ruling.

2006-07-14.title: Israel Vows to Crush Militia; Group’s Leader Is Defiant
2006-07-14.abstract: Israel today pressed ahead with its military operations in Lebanon, attacking the headquarters of Hezbollah’s leader, who called for “open war” with Israel.

2006-07-14.title: A Nation-Building Project Comes Apart in East Timor
2006-07-14.abstract: With the rebuilding effort in tatters, people are asking whether East Timor has any future at all as an independent nation.

2006-07-14.abstract: Also, a man claiming to represent Al Qaeda said they set up a Kashmir cell, and congratulated the conspirators.

2006-07-14.title: Violence Opens Old Wounds From Lebanon’s Past
2006-07-14.abstract: Many Lebanese began re-enacting rituals learned during 15 years of civil war, hunkering down for a protracted siege.

2006-07-14.abstract: President Bush’s position put him at odds with European allies on the eve of the annual Group of 8 summit meeting.

2006-07-16.title: In Haifa, a Scene of Death and Destruction
2006-07-16.abstract: HAIFA, Israel, July 16 — The train platform, slick with blood and littered with chunks of fallen roof, was the grim evidence confirming a prediction that Israeli security chiefs had made for years. Hezbollah, they said, had acquired larger, more powerful rockets that could soar over Israel’s thinly populated north and wreak havoc on major cities.

2006-07-16.title: Fishing Depletes Mediterranean Tuna, Conservationists Say

2006-07-17.abstract: Thousands of Israelis today remained holed up in bomb shelters that, while apparently rocket-proof, are hot and uncomfortable.

News agencies reported that at least 42 people were killed, including 12 riding in a minibus in Saida and 10 more on a bridge in the southern part of the country. A missile was said to strike a Lebanese army barracks north of Beirut.

2006-07-17.abstract: The U.S. on Sunday appeared to approve Israel's attack on Hezbollah, casting the widening conflict as part of a wider war on terrorism.

2006-07-19.abstract: Many Poles think the twins are trying to consolidate a shift toward right-wing, nationalist politics that has polarized Poland.

2006-07-20.abstract: The $130 million in relief funds will be given to Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, to avoid dealing with Hamas.

2006-07-20.abstract: The mass evacuations are threatening to overwhelm Cyprus, which was already saturated with vacationers during peak tourist season.

2006-07-20.abstract: Chinese authorities postponed the criminal trial of a rights lawyer today, as his supporters gathered in large numbers.

2006-07-21.abstract: Israel massed armored vehicles near its northern border today, suggesting that expanded ground operations may lie ahead. Hezbollah continued to rain rockets on northern Israel.

2006-07-22.abstract: After charges of censorship, the Indian government said a mysterious blockade on personal blogs would be repaired soon.

2006-07-22.title: Russia: Prison for Soldier Who Shot Chechen
2006-07-22.abstract: A Russian sniper who fatally shot a pro-Kremlin Chechen official last year was sentenced to four years in prison. The sniper, Sgt. Dmitry Arnautov, was convicted in a military court of “deliberately inflicting grave bodily harm leading to death,” according to Interfax. Sergeant Arnautov shot a Chechen State Council member, Sultan Demelkhanov, who was accompanied by armed body guards. Interfax quoted an official from the prosecutor’s office as saying that the sniper had “wrongly analyzed the situation.”.

2006-07-22.title: In 1990’s, Shadows Waged War
2006-07-22.abstract: The Hezbollah guerrilla campaign that drove Israel from Lebanon in 2000 was in many ways a precursor to the asymmetrical warfare Israeli troops would face again.

2006-07-22.abstract: The Bush administration is rushing a delivery of precision-guided bombs to Israel, which some military officers see as a sign of a longer campaign ahead.

2006-07-22.abstract: The conflict in Lebanon has ignited a robust debate on jihadist Web sites over backing for Hezbollah, a Shiite group.

2006-07-23.abstract: Israel’s preparation now seems to be less for a ground invasion than for more punishing airstrikes on Hezbollah military assets.

2006-07-23.abstract: In forced-labor camps in the far southeast, Italian authorities suspect torture, rape, forced prostitution and possibly killing occurred.

2006-07-24.abstract: Saddam Hussein was given a feeding tube on a day when widespread sectarian violence returned to Iraq after two days of relative calm.

2006-07-24.abstract: In continuing military exercises close to the Georgian border Sunday, Russian armed forces were trained to provide assistance to Russian peace keepers located in the Georgian conflict zones of Abkhazia and South Ossetia.

2006-07-25.abstract: Cyprus is doing an admirable job of handling the flood of people from Lebanon, but strains are showing.

2006-07-25.title: Refugees Cut Off From News of Village in Middle of Fight
2006-07-25.abstract: Few towns in Lebanon have paid a higher price than Aitaroun, where at least 30 residents have died, the deputy mayor said.

2006-07-25.title: After Reaching Outward, Poland Looks Back to Its Roots
2006-07-25.abstract: Neither the old nor the new members of the European Union seem to be celebrating what really is a historic achievement.

2006-07-25.title: In First Stop, Rice Confers With Leaders of Lebanon
2006-07-25.abstract: American diplomats said the visit demonstrated President Bush’s concern about the rising civilian death toll in Lebanon.

2006-07-25.title: With Hussein Still Boycotting Trial, a Relative Denies Guilt and Makes Speeches to the Judge
2006-07-25.abstract: The only defendant who showed up was Barzan Ibrahim al-Tikriti, Mr. Hussein’s half brother, who once directed the state security apparatus.

2006-07-26.title: Hussein Says He Prefers Firing Squad
2006-07-26.abstract: The trial of Saddam Hussein and his seven co-defendants resumed today.

2006-07-26.title: What’s Mannerly, Rowdy and Jabs Blair? Question Time
2006-07-26.abstract: At prime minister’s questions, the usual pugilistic gleam in Tony Blair's eye has been replaced by an aura of weariness, even defensiveness.

2006-07-26.title: Thailand: Election Officials Sent to Prison
2006-07-26.abstract: The Bangkok Criminal Court convicted the country’s three election commissioners of mishandling the parliamentary election in April by allowing unqualified candidates to run, and sentenced them to four years in prison. “We are more confident of fair elections now,” Abhisit Vejjajiva, the head of the Democrat Party, the main opposition, said in an interview. Opposition parties boycotted the election, which the party of Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra won overwhelmingly, and the courts later invalidated the vote. The three commissioners were widely seen as allies of Mr. Thaksin.

2006-07-26.title: A Lebanese Hospital Is an Unstable Pause From War
2006-07-26.abstract: Almost 1,600 people are trapped at the Tibnine Public Hospital, halfway between the hell of war and the promise of security.

2006-07-26.title: As the Price of Oil Soars, So Does Its Power to Shape Politics From Washington to Beijing
2006-07-26.abstract: Higher oil prices reduce the Bush administration’s maneuvering room overseas and frustrate American consumers at home.

2006-07-27.title: Aid Groups Are Criticized Over Tsunami Reconstruction
2006-07-27.abstract: In Indonesia, many donations from around the world have yet to materialize, and much of what has been spent has not been spent well.

2006-07-27.title: House Approves Nuclear Deal With India
2006-07-27.abstract: The deal would for the first time allow the U.S. to ship nuclear fuel and technology to a country that has refused to sign the Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty.

2006-07-27.title: Russia Makes New Attempt to Extradite Chechen Official
2006-07-27.abstract: In a further attempt to extradite Akhmed Zakayev, the rebel Chechen government representative, the Russian Prosecutor General on Wednesday charged him with inciting racial enmity and encouraging violence against ethnic Russians.

2006-07-27.title: U.N. Says It Protested to Israel for 6 Hours During Attack That Killed 4 Observers in Lebanon
2006-07-27.abstract: A U.N. official described the site as “well known and clearly marked” and added that no Hezbollah firing was taking place in the area during the period.

2006-07-27.title: Tyre Reels From Attacks That Never Fail to Shock
2006-07-27.abstract: The residents of Tyre, Lebanon, have suffered through numerous Israeli air strikes, and each attack makes them wonder anew why they are bombed.

2006-07-28.title: Chinese Workers Protest at Factory Making Toys for McDonald’s
2006-07-28.abstract: Rights groups accused the factory of violating Chinese labor laws, including provisions limiting overtime to 36 hours a month.

2006-07-28.title: Rice on the Defensive After Rome Summit
2006-07-28.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice has worked to resurrect the importance of traditional diplomacy, but the rewards of that hard work may have been undone.

2006-07-28.title: Russia Faulted in Captive’s Death in Chechnya
2006-07-28.abstract: The case, hailed by human rights groups as an important precedent, involved the disappearance and presumed death of a prisoner detained in Chechnya.

2006-07-28.title: For Hussein, a Long, Raucous Trial Ends in His Absence
2006-07-28.abstract: The verdict, to be announced on Oct. 16, could carry the possibility of death by hanging for Saddam Hussein.

2006-07-28.title: Israel Approves Call-Up, but Sets No Deployment
2006-07-28.abstract: The security cabinet ruled out a major military escalation for now, but the call-ups suggested Israel may be gearing up for a protracted battle.

2006-07-29.title: Sri Lanka: Peace Monitors Quit
2006-07-29.abstract: Finland and Denmark said they would withdraw their citizens from the five-country team that monitors a cease-fire between the government and the rebel Tamil Tigers. The Tigers had demanded the withdrawal of monitors belonging to the European Union after the bloc put the Tigers on its list of terrorist organizations in May. The pullout of the Finns and Danes reduces the size of the 57-member monitoring mission by about a third. Sweden, a European Union member, is keeping its nationals in the monitoring mission. Citizens of Norway and Iceland, neither of which belong to the Union, make up the rest of the mission, which investigates suspected violations of the 2002 truce, now all but shattered.

2006-07-29.title: U.N. Vote Becomes Referendum on U.S. Policy in Latin America
2006-07-29.abstract: Venezuela has set its sights on a seat on the Security Council and the United States has set out to block it.

2006-07-29.title: Rice Presses for Mideast Agreement
2006-07-29.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice returned to the region today to press for a cease-fire and the insertion of an international force along the Lebanese border with Israel.

2006-07-29.title: Knocking on Europe’s Door, Turkey Shows Growing Conservatism
2006-07-29.abstract: The prolonged road to membership has soured some attitudes toward the European Union, according to a recent survey.

2006-07-29.title: A Larger and More Powerful Rocket Hits Deeper in Israel, Adding a New Dimension
2006-07-29.abstract: The rockets, capable of carrying more than 200 pounds of explosives, fell more than 30 miles south of the Lebanese border.

2006-07-30.title: Images of Lebanese Death Intensify Arab Anger
2006-07-30.abstract: In downtown Beirut, thousands of demonstrators filled the streets, smashing windows at the United Nations headquarters.

2006-07-30.title: U.S. and Israel Shelved Laser as a Defense
2006-07-30.abstract: The U.S. and Israel shelved the experimental weapon because of its bulkiness, high costs and poor anticipated results on the battlefield.

2006-07-30.title: Pentagon Extends Tour for 4,000 Troops, Increasing Number in Iraq
2006-07-30.abstract: Military officials and analysts said the extension suggests that there will be no significant troop reductions in Iraq before the year’s end.

2006-07-30.title: Troops Move in Standoff in Sri Lanka
2006-07-30.abstract: The Sri Lankan Army’s move toward a canal blocked by rebels was the first ground troop advance since 2002.

2006-07-30.title: Iraqis Say They Deserve Aid for Fighting Terrorism
2006-07-30.abstract: Two high-ranking officials said that their country should be compensated with economic and military assistance.

2006-07-31.title: NATO Takes Over in Southern Afghanistan
2006-07-31.abstract: The difficult military role NATO is taking on was underscored by a bombing today that killed eight people.

2006-07-31.title: Sri Lanka and Rebels in Dispute Over Access to Irrigation Canal
2006-07-31.abstract: The Sri Lankan Army’s move toward a canal blocked by rebels was the first ground troop advance since 2002.

2006-07-31.title: Israeli Refugees Seek Friends and Families
2006-07-31.abstract: Israeli officials estimate that 300,000 northern Israelis have been displaced since fighting between Israel and Hezbollah began.

2006-07-31.title: French Foreign Minister to Meet Lebanese Leaders
2006-07-31.abstract: President Jacques Chirac of France has moved to lead the diplomatic campaign to end the crisis in Lebanon.

2006-07-31.title: U.N. Deplores Civilian Deaths, but Cease-Fire Call Is Blocked
2006-07-31.abstract: A Security Council statement deplored the civilian deaths in Qana and called for an “end to violence,” but not for an immediate cease-fire.
